name: Queen of Sheba Meatloaf
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Finely chopped fresh Italian parsley
1 pound Ground hot sausage, casing removed
3/4 cup Dry bulgur wheat, soaked for 30 minutes in water to cover
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Thoroughly coat a Bundt pan with olive oil.
3. In a large sauté pan, heat the olive oil and butter. Add the garlic and onions. Sauté until the onions are translucent, about 3 minutes. Add the parsley and fennel seeds.
4. Remove half the onion mixture and combine with the ground lamb in a large bowl. Set aside the other half of the onion mixture.
5. Add the ground sausage to the sauté pan, and brown. Add the lime juice and vermouth. Remove from heat. Add this mixture to the reserved onion mixture. Set aside.
6. Squeeze excess water from the bulgur wheat. Mix with the lamb. Add the eggs, ketchup, sesame oil, mint, allspice, rosemary, cinnamon, nutmeg, black pepper, and Worcestershire sauce. Mix well.
7. Divide this mixture into two portions. Press one portion into the pan. Arrange the sausage mixture on top of the lamb. Sprinkle the pine nuts and pistachios evenly over the sausage layer.
8. Press the other portion of the lamb mixture on top of the nut layer. Dot the loaf with the remaining tablespoon of butter. Place the broken bay leaves on top of the loaf.
9. Bake for 1 hour or until done. Remove the bay leaves. Let the loaf sit for 15 minutes before inverting it onto a round serving platter. Serves 8-10.
